message:
fix: address 6 bugs across networking, sync, trace, CLI, and web modules

DNS resolution (src/net/resolve.rs):
  Extract shared resolve_socket_addrs() helper that collects all results
  and rejects empty resolution sets with InvalidInput. Previously
  lookup_one used iterator .next() which discarded the error path for
  empty-but-successful resolution, and lookup_all had no empty check.
  Added tests for both empty-resolution rejection paths.

TCP connect (src/net/tcp/stream.rs):
  connect_with_timeout now resolves all addresses upfront and iterates
  through them, trying each with the correct address family (IPv4/IPv6)
  socket. Previously it resolved once and attempted a single address,
  which would fail on dual-stack hosts where the first address was
  unreachable. Last error is propagated if all addresses fail.

Mutex (src/sync/mutex.rs):
  Replace the 130-line duplicated OwnedLockFuture with a 3-line
  delegation through the borrowed MutexGuard path using ManuallyDrop to
  transfer lock ownership. The duplicated future was a maintenance hazard
  (any fix to LockFuture had to be mirrored) and this simplification is
  semantically equivalent — the borrowed guard's Drop is suppressed, so
  the lock stays held until the OwnedMutexGuard is dropped.

Trace replayer (src/trace/replayer.rs):
  Fix off-by-one in seek(): seeking to event_count() (one past the last
  event) is valid and positions the cursor at EOF with completed=true.
  Previously this returned UnexpectedEnd. Also fix empty-trace run()
  which previously never terminated because next() returned None without
  setting the completed flag.

Progress (src/cli/progress.rs):
  Clamp percentage to 100% when current exceeds total, preventing
  confusing >100% display in progress bars and TSV output.

Multipart (src/web/multipart.rs):
  Replace find_bytes with find_multipart_delimiter that validates the
  match starts on a line boundary and has a valid suffix (-- or CRLF).
  This prevents false boundary matches when the delimiter string appears
  embedded within a part's body content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
